title: Downtime Cost Calculator
description: Calculates the total cost of unplanned downtime based on lost production, labor, energy, quality, and recovery expenses, following industrial engineering standards (ISO 22400, Lean, Six Sigma).

inputs.label: Planned Production Rate
inputs.businessContext: The standard output rate per hour under normal operating conditions (ISO 22400).

businessContext_i18n.en: The standard output rate per hour under normal operating conditions (ISO 22400).

inputs.label: Downtime Duration
inputs.businessContext: Total duration of the unplanned downtime event.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total duration of the unplanned downtime event.

inputs.label: Revenue per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Selling price or standard cost per unit of output.

businessContext_i18n.en: Selling price or standard cost per unit of output.

inputs.label: Direct Labor Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Average hourly wage including benefits for operators affected by downtime.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average hourly wage including benefits for operators affected by downtime.

inputs.label: Energy Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Average cost of electricity, gas, or other energy consumed during production.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average cost of electricity, gas, or other energy consumed during production.

inputs.label: Scrap Rate During Downtime
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of material wasted due to restart or instability after downtime (Six Sigma defect rate).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of material wasted due to restart or instability after downtime (Six Sigma defect rate).

inputs.label: Recovery Time Factor
inputs.businessContext: Multiplier for additional time needed to return to steady-state production (Lean Takt time recovery).

businessContext_i18n.en: Multiplier for additional time needed to return to steady-state production (Lean Takt time recovery).

inputs.label: Shift Type
inputs.businessContext: Shift during which downtime occurred; affects labor cost multipliers (night/weekend premiums).

businessContext_i18n.en: Shift during which downtime occurred; affects labor cost multipliers (night/weekend premiums).

inputs.label: Include Quality Loss
inputs.businessContext: If true, adds cost of scrap and rework caused by the downtime event.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, adds cost of scrap and rework caused by the downtime event.

rules.message: Production rate and revenue per unit must be greater than zero.
Scrap rate must be between 0 and 100%.

thresholds.message: Downtime exceeds 4 hours – immediate escalation recommended.
Total downtime cost exceeds $10,000 – review root cause.
Scrap rate above 10% – investigate restart procedure.

formulas.label: Lost Production Units
formulas.description: Units not produced due to downtime.

formulas.label: Lost Revenue
formulas.description: Revenue lost from units not produced.

formulas.label: Labor Cost
formulas.description: Direct labor cost incurred during downtime (idle time).
formulas.expression: direct_labor_cost_per_hour * downtime_duration * (CASE shift_type WHEN 'night' THEN 1.15 WHEN 'weekend' THEN 1.5 ELSE 1.0 END)

formulas.label: Energy Cost
formulas.description: Energy consumed during downtime (often idle but still running).

formulas.label: Quality Loss Cost
formulas.description: Cost of scrap and rework due to downtime (if include_quality_loss is true).
formulas.expression: CASE WHEN include_quality_loss = true THEN (lost_production_units * (scrap_rate_during_downtime / 100) * revenue_per_unit * 0.5) ELSE 0 END

formulas.label: Recovery Cost
formulas.description: Additional cost to return to steady-state (overtime, expedited maintenance).

formulas.label: Total Downtime Cost
formulas.description: Primary result – sum of all cost components.

primary.label: Total Downtime Cost
primary.description: Aggregate cost of the downtime event.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Lost Throughput Ratio
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Fraction of weekly capacity lost (WERC benchmark).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Cost per Downtime Hour
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Average cost per hour of downtime.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Opportunity Cost (Lost Profit)
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated lost profit assuming 20% margin (Lean accounting).

suggestedActions.label: Implement TPM (Total Productive Maintenance)
suggestedActions.description: Reduce unplanned downtime by 30-50% through autonomous and planned maintenance (Lean TPM).

suggestedActions.label: Standardize Restart Procedures
suggestedActions.description: Create checklists to reduce scrap rate and recovery time (Six Sigma DMAIC).

suggestedActions.label: Install Real-Time Monitoring
suggestedActions.description: Use IIoT sensors to detect anomalies before downtime occurs (Industry 4.0).

suggestedActions.label: Cross-Train Operators
suggestedActions.description: Ensure backup personnel to minimize labor idle cost (Lean workforce flexibility).

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Total Cost
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total cost adjusted by a confidence factor (0.8 to 1.2) based on input reliability.
